summary: Patrick reports progress on his 30-day sprint to build a widget product, having solved a key technical challenge around making widgets viral using a modified Lightbox library. He has a working JavaScript front-end prototype but needs to build the back end. His weekend plan is to create a Bingo Card Creator-specific widget as a proof of concept that can drive immediate business value while he continues building the full widget creation platform.
key_points: Found an open-source solution (Lightbox Gone Wild) that solved the core virality challenge by displaying widget source code inline without redirecting visitors away from the host page
Strategic approach of building a specific widget for his existing product first to generate business value before the general platform is ready
Acknowledges the long tail of work beyond the core product: account management, billing, analytics, marketing copy, and scalability
money_quotes: I *love it* when other people do my work for me.
Plus, score, it will start driving business value for me even without having the widget creator website up.
No point in being viral if you can't tell how viral you are.
key_insight: When building a platform product, start by creating a specific instance for your own existing business to validate the concept and generate immediate value before tackling the general case.
